---
title: "Clonezilla unable to mount /home/partimag"
date: 2013-02-21
forum: Hardware
---

### Post by D0natell0 on 2013-02-21
Hi,
I'm running a USB stick with current stable clonezilla-live (clonezilla-live-2.0.1-15-i686-pae.iso). When choosing to use a local device for /home/partimag I get an error "Unable to mount resource" (see image).

[IMG]http://ubuntuforums.org/data:image/jpeg;base64,[/IMG]I've been using Clonezilla for about 1 year for system building and only recently got this problem. From the error message, and html tags, it looks like somethings corrupted somewhere. I tried with an updated install of the Clonezilla USB stick to no avail. 

The drive I'm trying to copy the image from is an external 120Gb Usb drive.

Target system:-
CPU: Intel Core-i7 2620M
RAM: 2x Kingston 8Gb 1333Mhz
Motherboard: QM77

Thanks in advance.

---

### Post by D0natell0 on 2013-02-22
bump

---

### Post by schragge on 2013-02-22
On the *Start Clonezilla* screen, select *Enter_shell* from menu, then *(2) Enter command line prompt*.

At the command line, run the following commands:
```

grep ^[^#] /etc/ocs/ocs-live.conf
grep ^ocs_prerun /etc/drbl/drbl-ocs.conf
cat /proc/cmdline
blkid

``` and post the output here.

---

### Post by D0natell0 on 2013-02-22
Hi, I wasn't sure how best to capture the output, so I'm using photos.
Thanks for your help.

---

### Post by schragge on 2013-02-22
Well, I guess */dev/sda1 LABEL="KINGSTON"* is your USB stick with Clonezilla Live. Your external hard drive doesn't show up in the output of *blkid*.

Could you please show the output of
```

sudo parted -l
dmesg | grep '] sd '

```

---

### Post by D0natell0 on 2013-02-22
There is some sort of error on the external 120GB USB drive, please see images.
Thanks.

---

### Post by schragge on 2013-02-22
Look at [this thread]("https://bbs.archlinux.org/viewtopic.php?id=125831"). Can you try to edit the grub menu when booting Clonezilla, and add *usb-storage.delay_use=5* as recommended there?

---

### Post by D0natell0 on 2013-02-22
I wasn't 100% sure if I understood, but I edited the grub menu and added the parameter *usb-storage.delay_use=5 *as I think you suggested[I]. 

[/I]I booted through from there (the USB drive was already attached). From the command line, I tried the following, but the USB drive didn't show up.

```
dmesg | grep '] sd '
```I tried unplugging and re-plugging the USB drive and got the same errors as before.

So I tried using 10 for the delay parameter, but still no use :(
Maybe I've misunderstood?
Thanks.

---

### Post by schragge on 2013-02-22
If you did it right then
```
cat /sys/module/usb_storage/parameters/delay_use
```
should show **5**.

But it's also possible that the wrong delay is not your problem at all. [Here](http://forums.gentoo.org/viewtopic.php?p=6871586) is another thread where they blame it on buggy USB-to-SATA interface in the enclosure. What model is your external HDD? What does *lsusb -v* show for it? Compare the output of *dmesg* on your machine to what is shown in the thread. Do you get similar error messages?

---
